children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: There is established in the Copyright Office the Copyright Claims Board, which shall serve as an alternative forum in which parties may voluntarily seek to resolve certain copyright claims regarding any category of copyrighted work, as provided in this chapter.

children.heading: .—Copyright claims officers
children.label: 1 .—Copyright claims officers
children.text: The Register of Copyrights shall recommend 3 full-time Copyright Claims Officers to serve on the Copyright Claims Board in accordance with paragraph (3)(A). The Officers shall be appointed by the Librarian of Congress to such positions after consultation with the Register of Copyrights.

children.heading: .—Copyright claims attorneys
children.label: 2 .—Copyright claims attorneys
children.text: The Register of Copyrights shall hire not fewer than 2 full-time Copyright Claims Attorneys to assist in the administration of the Copyright Claims Board.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: Each Copyright Claims Officer shall be an attorney who has not fewer than 7 years of legal experience.

children.label: I
children.text: have substantial experience in the evaluation, litigation, or adjudication of copyright infringement claims; and

children.label: II
children.text: between those 2 Officers, have represented or presided over a diversity of copyright interests, including those of both owners and users of copyrighted works.

children.heading: .—Alternative dispute resolution
children.label: iii .—Alternative dispute resolution
children.text: The Copyright Claims Officer not described in clause (ii) shall have substantial familiarity with copyright law and experience in the field of alternative dispute resolution, including the resolution of litigation matters through that method of resolution.

children.heading: .—Copyright claims attorneys
children.label: B .—Copyright claims attorneys
children.text: Each Copyright Claims Attorney shall be an attorney who has not fewer than 3 years of substantial experience in copyright law.

children.heading: .—Definition
children.label: i .—Definition
children.text: In this subparagraph, the term “senior level employee of the Federal Government” means an employee, other than an employee in the Senior Executive Service, the position of whom is classified above GS–15 of the General Schedule.

children.heading: .—Pay range
children.label: ii .—Pay range
children.text: Each Copyright Claims Officer shall be compensated at a rate of pay that is not less than the minimum, and not more than the maximum, rate of pay payable for senior level employees of the Federal Government, including locality pay, as applicable.

children.heading: .—Copyright claims attorneys
children.label: B .—Copyright claims attorneys
children.text: Each Copyright Claims Attorney shall be compensated at a rate of pay that is not more than the maximum rate of pay payable for level 10 of GS–15 of the General Schedule, including locality pay, as applicable.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), a Copyright Claims Officer shall serve for a renewable term of 6 years.

children.label: i
children.text: The first such Copyright Claims Officer appointed shall be appointed for a term of 4 years.

children.label: ii
children.text: The second Copyright Claims Officer appointed shall be appointed for a term of 5 years.

children.label: iii
children.text: The third Copyright Claims Officer appointed shall be appointed for a term of 6 years.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: If a vacancy occurs in the position of a Copyright Claims Officer, the Librarian of Congress shall, upon the recommendation of, and in consultation with, the Register of Copyrights, act expeditiously to appoint a Copyright Claims Officer for that position.

children.heading: .—Vacancy before expiration
children.label: ii .—Vacancy before expiration
children.text: An individual appointed to fill a vacancy occurring before the expiration of the term for which the predecessor of the individual was appointed shall be appointed to serve a 6-year term.

children.heading: .—Incapacity
children.label: B .—Incapacity
children.text: If a Copyright Claims Officer is temporarily unable to perform the duties of the Officer, the Librarian of Congress shall, upon recommendation of, and in consultation with, the Register of Copyrights, act expeditiously to appoint an interim Copyright Claims Officer to perform such duties during the period of such incapacity.

children.heading: .—Sanction or removal
children.label: 7 .—Sanction or removal
children.text: Subject to section 1503(b), the Librarian of Congress may sanction or remove a Copyright Claims Officer.

children.heading: .—Administrative support
children.label: 8 .—Administrative support
children.text: The Register of Copyrights shall provide the Copyright Claims Officers and Copyright Claims Attorneys with necessary administrative support, including technological facilities, to carry out the duties of the Officers and Attorneys under this chapter.

children.heading: .—Location of copyright claims board
children.label: 9 .—Location of copyright claims board
children.text: The offices and facilities of the Copyright Claims Officers and Copyright Claims Attorneys shall be located at the Copyright Office.
